fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 61 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective`), so when `now` exactly equals the TTL the entry is incorrectly treated as still valid rather than expired.

constraints.intent: When now equals effective the branch should trigger (return None), so the condition now > effective must be replaced by now >= effective; this expr is false when now == effective and the branch is not taken.
Capture that when now equals effective__1 the subsequent expiry check must return None — the strict inequality fails to handle this boundary.
After the else branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than effective; if now == effective we should have taken the then-branch, so this being false reveals the off-by-one boundary bug.
If now is at or past the TTL, the function must return None; failing tests show it returns the payload instead.
